best_image_source: Esri World Imagery aerial photo provided in prompt
roof_system: Mixed roof types: TPO, EPDM or Modified Bitumen, Metal

possible_roof_systems.evidence: Large upper roof field is bright white and smooth with faint broad sheet layout; seams are not crisply resolved, so TPO is the conservative default for the dominant light membrane-like surface.

The mid/lower central dark roof block reads as a dark matte low-slope field with an asphaltic look; image softness prevents confirming roll laps, but the tone and roof-field character fit modified bitumen.
The same dark roof block could also be a broad dark single-ply membrane; flat matte appearance and unresolved seams leave EPDM plausible.
The small east/southeast attached strip shows straighter segmented geometry and panel-like divisions compared with the membrane roofs, which is most consistent with a metal roof field.

roof_age_estimate: Likely mid-life to older on the dark roof sections; the bright white upper membrane appears newer or restored. Exact age cannot be determined from aerial imagery alone.

notes: Upper white roof shows scattered discoloration and small stains but no clear widespread ponding.
Dark central/lower roof has uneven color and weathering consistent with higher leak-potential serviceability risk.
Multiple rooftop units, vents, and curbs increase flashing and seal risk across the larger roof fields.
No obvious tree overhang or loose debris accumulation is visible in the roof field.

observations: Visible risk factors include dark staining or inconsistent roof color, high penetration or rooftop-unit density.
Large dominant upper roof field is bright white and smooth, consistent with a single-ply membrane or coated surface; TPO is the conservative label from aerial evidence.
Mid/lower central roof block is darker, matte, and visually distinct from the upper roof, supporting a separate dark membrane or asphaltic roof zone.
Small attached east/southeast roof strip shows straighter panel-like divisions and rigid geometry, supporting a separate metal section.
Scattered small stains/discoloration are visible on the white roof; no clear extensive ponding signature is resolved.
summary: Aerial imagery dated not provided shows a mixed commercial roof with a dominant bright white low-slope membrane field, a separate dark low-slope roof section, and a small attached metal roof strip. Visible risk factors include dark staining or inconsistent roof color, high penetration or rooftop-unit density.
recommendation: Have a qualified commercial roof-coating contractor or roof consultant perform an onsite inspection, moisture testing where warranted, confirm the exact systems, document repairs, and provide a reliable project-price proposal. If the dominant white low-slope roof is otherwise restorable and not asphalt shingle, evaluate whether a silicone roof restoration is appropriate before a costlier overlay or replacement; a properly designed silicone system can reduce tear-off waste, cost, and disruption, create a seamless monolithic white reflective cool-roof surface, improve resistance to UV exposure and hail, withstand ponding water when properly designed and installed, and may qualify for a manufacturer warranty of up to 20 years. If inspection reveals widespread moisture, failed insulation, deck deterioration, or an incompatible substrate, pursue overlay or replacement evaluation instead.

limitations: Seams and laps are not crisply resolved
Aerial imagery cannot separate TPO from PVC or coating with certainty

supporting_cues: Distinct from adjacent white membrane zone
limitations: Roll laps and seam texture are not resolved well enough to choose confidently between EPDM and modified bitumen

supporting_cues: More rigid construction than adjacent membrane roofs
limitations: Raised rib height and metal edge details are not fully resolved

candidates.evidence: Bright white smooth low-slope field with apparent broad sheet layout; seams are not crisply resolved enough to separate TPO from PVC or coating, so TPO is the conservative default for a light membrane-like surface.
Uniform bright white membrane-like appearance could also fit PVC, but the image does not resolve enough surface detail to confirm it over TPO.
A reflective coated roof is possible from the smooth white appearance, but there is no clear evidence of patchy application or substrate telegraphing.

limitations: Aerial resolution is limited; seams, laps, and application texture are not clearly resolved.
White surface alone is insufficient to distinguish TPO from PVC or coating.
Confidence capped because the image does not resolve the fine details needed for a stronger material call.

candidates.evidence: Dark low-slope roof with a more asphaltic/matte appearance than the upper white field; material detail is too soft to confirm roll laps, but the tone and roof-field character better fit modified bitumen than metal.
A dark, flat membrane remains plausible because the imagery does not resolve enough texture or seam detail to separate it from modified bitumen.
Metal is less likely because no crisp raised ribs, rigid panel edges, or clear reflective metal geometry are resolved.

limitations: Panel ribs, roll laps, and edge detailing are not resolved well enough to separate dark membrane alternatives confidently.
Confidence capped due to soft imagery and ambiguity among dark low-slope systems.

roof_zones.location: small east/southeast lower attached roof strip

candidates.evidence: The strip shows a more rigid, segmented roof geometry with straight field divisions and panel-like rectangular modules compared with the adjacent membrane roofs, which is most consistent with a metal roof field.
If the apparent divisions are artifact or attachment rows rather than actual panel geometry, a darker modified-bitumen roof remains possible, but the roof-scale pattern is less supportive.
A dark membrane is a weaker fallback only because the image softness prevents fully resolving whether the straight lines are seams or metal paneling.

limitations: Fine rib height and fastening details are not fully resolved, so metal subtype is not assigned.
Image softness limits confidence, though the geometry is more supportive of metal than the other visible roof zones.

overall_limitations: Reference imagery is withheld, so only aerial cues are available.
Roof field detail is moderate to soft; seam/rib/texture evidence is limited in multiple zones.
Overall confidence is constrained by the weakest zone calls and should be treated conservatively.
